description_blurb: This file contains memorandums, correspondence, and forms related to the reporting of information on flying discs and investigations into sightings.
title: 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1
incident_location: Hobson, Ohio; Bakersfield, California; Houston, Texas
summary: This file contains memorandums, correspondence, and forms related to the reporting of information on flying discs and investigations into sightings. It includes reports of multiple sightings in Hobson, Ohio, Bakersfield, California, and Houston, Texas, with descriptions of round, phosphorescent objects and falling aircraft-like objects. The document also details Air Force policy on collecting and evaluating UAP information, and discusses the unfeasibility of maintaining continuous fighter aircraft alerts for interception, as well as references to German flying wing designs.
reported_object_description: Multiple objects described: a round, phosphorescent object appearing 9 inches in diameter from ground level; two objects similar to falling aircraft with smoke and debris, one on fire with red and black smoke; and general 'Flying Disks'.
reported_behavior: High speed movement, phosphorescent trail, falling to earth from an unknown source.
official_resolution: For the Hobson, Ohio incident, the reporting headquarters had no further information and was not initiating investigation. For the Bakersfield, California incidents, an investigation was initiated, but searching parties failed to locate the objects.
bust_reasoning: The document contains multiple visual reports. The Hobson, Ohio sighting is a single-sensor (visual) report with vague characteristics ('phosphorescent trail', 'great speed') that could be explained by a meteor or atmospheric phenomenon. However, the Bakersfield, California incidents involve multiple witnesses observing 'falling aircraft' with smoke and fire, followed by an *unsuccessful search* by 'searching parties, aircraft and rescue units' to locate any wreckage. This failure to find physical evidence for what appeared to be falling aircraft is a feature that resists easy prosaic explanation, pushing the score below 65. While not multi-sensor, the multi-witness convergence and the anomalous outcome of the search place it in the 'genuinely uncertain' to 'resists easy explanation' category.

GOVERNMENT PRINTING OFFICE # 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1 - Page 2 ~~CONFIDENTIAL~~ HEADQUARTERS ELEVENTH AIR FORCE 1612 South Cameron Street PAFD Harrisburg, Pennsylvania SUBJECT: Report of "Flying Discs" TO: Chief of Staff United States Air Force Washington 25, D. C. ATTN: Director of Intelligence, Requirements Division 1. In compliance with paragraph 1a, ADC Letter 45-5, 25 March 1948, the following information relative to "Flying Discs" is hereby submitted. The attached report was received this Headquarters 27 May 1948, from Special Agent, D.K. Brown, Federal Bureau of Investigation, Cleveland, Ohio. This Headquarters has no further information and is not initiating any investigation in accordance with paragraph 2, ADC ltr. 45-5. Any additional information received by this Headquarters will be immediately forwarded to your Headquarters. Copies of report have been forwarded to Hq. Air Materiel Command and Hq. Air Defense Command as directed in above mentioned directive. a. Location and time of sighting - Hobson, Ohio; night of 8 May 48. b. Weather at the time - unknown by this Hq. c. Names, occupations, and addresses of witnesses - Addresses Ben Rupe, New York Central System Car Inspector unknown. Earl Roush, " " " " " " " " " " Bob White, Yard Clerk, N.Y.C. C. K. Hite, Patrolman, N.Y.C. d. Photographs of objects, if available - unknown this Hq. e. Sketches of objects configuration - unknown this Hq. f. Objects sighted: (1) Number - not known this Headquarters (2) Shape - round (3) Size - appeared to be nine inches in diameter from ground level. ~~CONFIDENTIAL~~ # 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1 - Page 3 ~~CONFIDENTIAL~~ EAFD Subj: Report of "Flying Discs" (4) Color - phosphorescent (5) Speed e Great amount of speed (6) Heading - 90° (7) Manueverability - not known (8) Altitude - 6 to 8 miles (9) Sound - unknown to this Hq. GOVERNMENT PRINTING OFFICE # 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1 - Page 5 ~~SECRET~~ AIR MATERIEL COMMAND TSNAD-2B/ACL:ccc Wright Field, Dayton, O. 24 September 1947 TSNAD-2B "Flying Disc" Commanding General Army Air Forces Washington 25, D. C. ATTN: AC/AS-2 Major General George McDonald 1. In compliance with General McDonald's telephone conversation, this date, to Colonel H. M. McCoy, T-2, Hq. AMC, attached herewith is a copy of a drawing entitled, "Loedding Flying Disc", designated LD-2. Because of patent rights involved, it is requested that a record be kept of all persons reviewing this drawing by witnessing with signatures and dates directly on the drawing. 2. This drawing should be returned to this office when it has served its purpose so that it can be turned over to the Patent Office Hq. AMC for official record. 3. Inclosed herewith is also a report prepared by the Royal Aircraft Establishment, Technical Note, AERO 1703, which describes the Horten tailless aircraft. The following references are invited which is considered significant in relating the Horten brothers perspective thinking and accomplishments toward the alleged "Flying Saucer" case: a. Page 5, Paragraph 4 and 6. b. Page 6, Paragraph 1 and 7. c. Page 26, Paragraph 3 and 10. d. Page 27, Paragraph 12. e. Page 28, Paragraphs 1 through 5. f. Page 54, Photograph of Horten "Parabola". ~~SECRET~~ U.39942 2-44 # 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1 - Page 6 ~~SECRET~~ Basic ltr, dtd 24 Sep 47, to CG, Hq AAF, Wash, subj: "Flying Disc" g. Page 69, The Horten VIII. h. Pages 71 and 72, Photographs of the Horten IX Versions. i. Page 74, Drawing of the "Parabola". This document should also be returned when it has served its intended purpose. 4. A recent report from the U. S. Military attache, Moscow, USSR, dated 9 June 1947, indicates that 1800 aircraft, directly or indirectly based on the Horten VIII design (six engine pusher, having a wing spread of 131 feet and a gross weight of approximately thirty-three thousand pounds) are being built for bomber squadron use. The Russian version, however, is jet propelled, reference report whose identification No. is V-32291-S. 5. The T-2 report "German Flying Wings Designed by Horten Brothers", No. F-SU-1110-Nd, also inclosed herewith, may be retained by your office if desired. FOR THE COMMANDING GENERAL: sgd H. M. McCOY Colonel, Air Corps Deputy Commanding General Intelligence (T-2) 3 Incls: 1. Drawing 2. RAE Tech Note, AERO 1703 3. Attn: Plans & Collection Branch
Lt.Col. Smith | HQ. USAF - AFOIR | 27 FEB 1948 | Lt.Col.Garrett/dk/4544 | 1. It is Air Force policy not to ignore reports of sightings and phenomena in the atmosphere, but to recognize that part of its mission is to collect, collate, evaluate, and act on information of this nature. 2. In implementing this policy the Air Materiel Command has been designated the Air Force agency to collect, collate, evaluate, and distribute to interested government agencies and contractors all information concerning sightings and phenomena in the atmosphere which can be construed to be of concern to the national security. 3. All Air Force installations within and outside Zone of Interior and Alaska have been instructed to report all sightings, or information concerning sightings which comes to their attention, direct to the Commanding General, Air Materiel Command, Wright-Patterson Air Force Base, Dayton, Ohio, Attn: MCI. 4. Air Materiel Command has been authorized direct contact with all Air Force installations whenever required to develop additional information on the flying discs. 5. It is requested that all Department of the Army installations also be instructed to report such occurrences directly to Air Materiel Command and that they be informed that Air Materiel Command is authorized to contact them direct when necessary in connection with such occurrences. 6. If possible, reports to Air Materiel Command should include the following information: a. Location and time of sighting b. Weather at the time c. Names, occupations, and addresses of witnesses d. Photographs of objects, if available e. Object sighted: (1) Number (4) Color (7) Maneuverability (10) Exhaust trail (2) Shape (5) Speed (8) Altitude or not (3) Size (6) Heading (9) Sound g. General remarks FOR THE CHIEF OF STAFF: [Signature: C. P. Cabell] C. P. CABELL Major General, U.S.A.F. Chief, Air Intel. Req. Div. GOVERNMENT PRINTING OFFICE # 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1 - Page 16 C O P Y ~~CONFIDENTIAL~~ HEADQUARTERS FOURTH AIR FORCE Office of the Assistant Chief of Staff, A-2 Intelligence Hamilton Field, California 4AFDA-3/1208-I Mar 11, 1948 SUBJECT: Investigation of Flying Disc. TO: Chief of Staff United States Air Forces Washington, D. C. ATTN: Directorate of Intelligence Air Intelligence Requirements Division 1. Incident reported on 9 March 1948, this headquarters, per telephone call from Sgt. A. M. Larsen, Sheriff's office, Bakersfield, California. a. Observation by Mr. Les Buchner, Bakersfield, California, of two objects falling to earth from unknown source on 5 March 1948 between 1610 and 1655 hours. Description of objects similar to falling aircraft with smoke and debris trailing. Observation made at Bakersfield with objects sighted southwest toward Buena Vista Lake, California. b. Observation by Mr. Denio, employee of the Pacific General Electric Company, Bakersfield, California, of two objects which fell to earth from unknown source north of Bakersfield, California, 8 March 1948. One object seemed to be on fire with red and black smoke trailing behind. 2. Informant Larsen stated that searching parties, aircraft and rescue units have made numerous attempts to locate these reported objects without success. 3. Investigation of incident has been initiated by this headquarters. Report will follow. /s/ DONALD L. SPRINGER Lt. Colonel, USAF AC of S, A-2 Information to: ADC, Mitchel Fld, N.Y. FBI, San Francisco G-2, Sixth Army DIO, San Francisco (12th Nav. Dist) ~~CONFIDENTIAL~~ # 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1 - Page 17 AFOIR-CO-5/Lt.Col.Garrett/dk/ 4544 9 March 48 [SECRET] AFOIR-CO-5 17 MAR 1948 SUBJECT: Flying Discs TO: Commanding General Air Materiel Command Wright-Patterson Air Force Base Dayton, Ohio ATTN: MCI 1. Reference is made to Colonel McCoy's informal proposal that certain fighter aircraft be maintained on a continuous alert status, within the Zone of the Interior, to aid in gathering information on Flying Discs. 2. This proposal is considered unfeasible for the following reasons: a. The outlay of aircraft and personnel would be too great in relation to the results obtained. b. Proper interception is not possible, except by accident, without complete radar coverage. The Air Force is incapable of providing such coverage. c. It is doubtful if fighter aircraft would be able to follow up reports emanating, for the most part, from civilian sources. BY COMMAND OF THE CHIEF OF STAFF: [Stamp: HQ USAF, 17 MAR 1948, OAS-MAIL ROOM] GEORGE C. MCDONALD Major General, USAF Director of Intelligence, Office of Deputy Chief of Staff, Operations P 99846 [Handwritten note, vertical, right margin]: 000.9 Plum[illegible] [Handwritten note, bottom right]: P80 recommended above answer by RBR Cont 2 3 Mar to D/I USAF | OFFICE SYMBOL | AFOIR-CO | AFOIR | AFOIN | | | | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | | GRADE AND SURNAME OF COORDINATING OFFICERS | Col. Taylor III
LtCol.Garrett | C. P. CABELL
Maj. Gen., USAF | [illegible] | | | 16-20242-2 U. S. GOVERNMENT PRINTING OFFICE # 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1 - Page 18 AF HQ-33 (30 MAY 44) HEADQUARTERS ARMY AIR FORCES ROUTING AND RECORD SHEET [Stamp: SECRET] | TALLY NO. | 080 | | :--- | :--- | | FILE NO. | | SUBJECT: Flying Discs TO: Director of Plans and Operations DATE: 12 FEB 1948 FROM: Director of Intelligence COMMENT NO. 1 Lt. Col Garrett/lwd/4544 1. By letter from this Headquarters, dated 30 December 1947 with subject as above, the Commanding General, Air Materiel Command is instructed to "set up a project whose purpose is to collect, collate, evaluate and distribute to interested government agencies and contractors all information concerning sightings and phenomena in the atmosphere which can be construed to be of concern to the national security". 2. The Commanding General, Air Materiel Command feels that the responsibility assigned to him for collecting and developing information and intelligence on the "flying discs" should be complemented by a requirement that all Air Force installations in the United States and Alaska provide a minimum of one each fighter or night interceptor type aircraft, with necessary crews, on a continuous alert basis. These aircraft should be equipped with gun camera, and such armament as deemed advisable, in order to secure photographs necessary to the obtainment of all possible data on any reported and sighted unusual phenomena, of the "flying disc" type, in the atmosphere. 3. It is envisaged that each Air Force installation maintaining aircraft on an alert basis for this purpose would investigate all unusual flying objects reported from any source in any area over which it has cognizance. Such a program would obviously require a detailed Standard Operating Procedure, in the preparation of which Air Materiel Command should cooperate. 4. Your comments and/or concurrence are requested. [Signature: C.P. Cabell] C. P. CABELL Brigadier General, U.S.A.F. Chief, Air Intel. Req. Div. Directorate of Intelligence 1/13 PAGE [SECRET stamp] 3-1109, AF (REV 20 MAY 44) (62) # 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1 - Page 19 AF HQ-33 20 MAY 44) DEPARTMENT OF THE AIR FORCE [SECRET] ~~HEADQUARTERS ARMY AIR FORCES~~ ROUTING AND RECORD SHEET | TALLY NO. | air | | :--- | :--- | | FILE NO. | | SUBJECT: Flying Discs TO: Director of Intelligence DATE: 3 MAR 1948 COMMENT No. 2 FROM: Director of Plans and Operations Major Cook:csk 72233 1. The proposal of Air Materiel Command for stationing fighter aircraft at all bases on a continuous alert status is not considered feasible for the following reasons: a. The outlay of aircraft and personnel would be too great consistent with the results to be obtained. b. Proper interception is not possible, except by accident, without complete [illegible]dar coverage which the Air Force is not capable of providing. c. It is doubtful if fighter aircraft would be able to follow up reports emanating, for the most part, from civilian sources. 2. It is recommended that Air Materiel Command's responsibility for "collecting" information on unusual phenomena in the atmosphere be confined to the establishment of direct channels for the receipt of such information. 3. It is further recommended that in lieu of the proposal of Air Materiel Command, all other major commands be directed to cooperate with Air Materiel Command and to channel information directly to that command. [Handwritten signature: S. E. Anderson] S. E. ANDERSON Major General, U.S.A.F. Director of Plans and Operations. GOVERNMENT PRINTING OFFICE # 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1 - Page 21 CONFIDENTIAL HEADQUARTERS TENTH AIR FORCE BROOKS FIELD, SAN ANTONIO, TEXAS D 333.5 7 January 1948 SUBJECT: "Flying Disks" TO: Chief of Staff United States Air Force Washington 25, D. cover_up_reasoning: The document is replete with 'CONFIDENTIAL' and 'SECRET' markings, indicating a high level of classification for internal communications. More significantly, page 4 explicitly states that a 'T-2 Report 'German Flying Wings Designed by Horten Brothers'' was 'retained by this office' from the recipient, indicating information control. Page 5 details strict control over a 'Loedding Flying Disc' drawing, requiring a 'record be kept of all persons reviewing this drawing by witnessing with signatures and dates directly on the drawing.' Pages 5-6 also discuss highly sensitive intelligence regarding Russian development of jet-propelled aircraft based on German designs in the context of 'Flying Discs.' Finally, page 17 references an 'informal proposal that certain fighter aircraft be maintained on a continuous alert status... to aid in gathering information on Flying Discs,' which points to a substantial, concealed operational response being considered. These elements go beyond routine classification, demonstrating substantial concealment of the nature and scope of the investigation and the intelligence being considered.
summary_zh: 本文件包含关于飞碟信息报告及目击事件调查的备忘录、往来函件及表格。内容包括俄亥俄州霍布森、加利福尼亚州贝克斯菲尔德和得克萨斯州休斯顿的多起目击报告，描述了圆形、发磷光物体以及坠落的类似飞行器物体。文件还详细阐述了空军关于收集和评估UAP信息的政策，讨论了维持战斗机持续警戒以进行拦截的不可行性，并提及德国飞翼设计。
reported_object_description_zh: 描述了多种物体：一个圆形、发磷光物体，从地面看直径约9英寸；两个类似坠落飞行器的物体，伴有烟雾和碎片，其中一个着火，冒着红黑烟雾；以及普遍的“飞碟”。
reported_behavior_zh: 高速移动，伴有磷光轨迹，从未知来源坠落至地面。
official_resolution_zh: 关于俄亥俄州霍布森事件，报告总部未获得进一步信息，且未启动调查。关于加利福尼亚州贝克斯菲尔德事件，已启动调查，但搜寻队未能找到相关物体。
bust_reasoning_zh: 该文件包含多份视觉报告。俄亥俄州霍布森的目击事件是一份单传感器（视觉）报告，其模糊特征（“磷光轨迹”、“高速”）可用流星或大气现象解释。然而，加利福尼亚州贝克斯菲尔德事件涉及多名目击者观察到伴有烟雾和火焰的“坠落飞行器”，随后“搜寻队、飞机和救援单位”未能成功搜寻到任何残骸。未能找到看似坠落飞行器的物理证据，这一特点难以用普通解释轻易说明，使评分低于65分。尽管并非多传感器，但多名目击者证词一致以及搜寻结果异常，使其归入“真正不确定”至“难以轻易解释”的类别。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件遍布“机密”和“秘密”标记，表明内部通信的高度机密性。更重要的是，第4页明确指出，“T-2报告‘霍顿兄弟设计的德国飞翼’”被“本办公室从接收方处保留”，表明信息受到控制。第5页详细说明了对“Loedding飞碟”图纸的严格控制，要求“所有查阅此图纸的人员必须在图纸上直接签名并注明日期以作记录”。第5-6页还讨论了在“飞碟”背景下，关于俄罗斯基于德国设计开发喷气式飞机的极敏感情报。最后，第17页提及一项“非正式提议，即某些战斗机应保持持续警戒状态……以协助收集飞碟信息”，这表明正在考虑一项重大、隐蔽的行动响应。这些要素超越了常规分类，表明对调查的性质、范围以及正在审议的情报进行了实质性隐瞒。
incident_location_zh: 俄亥俄州霍布森；加利福尼亚州贝克斯菲尔德；得克萨斯州休斯顿
claims: Round, phosphorescent object sighted in Hobson, Ohio, appearing 9 inches in diameter from ground level, moving at high speed with a phosphorescent trail.
Two objects, similar to falling aircraft with smoke and debris, sighted in Bakersfield, California, with one appearing on fire with red and black smoke.
Air Force policy established to collect, collate, evaluate, and distribute information on 'Flying Discs' through Air Materiel Command.
Proposal for continuous fighter aircraft alert for UAP interception deemed unfeasible due to cost, lack of radar coverage, and difficulty following civilian reports.
References to German 'Flying Wings' designed by Horten Brothers and a 'Loedding Flying Disc' (LD-2) as potential explanations or related designs.
claims_zh: 俄亥俄州霍布森目击到一个圆形、发磷光物体，从地面看直径约9英寸，高速移动并伴有磷光轨迹。
加利福尼亚州贝克斯菲尔德目击到两个类似坠落飞行器的物体，伴有烟雾和碎片，其中一个着火，冒着红黑烟雾。
空军已制定政策，通过空军物资司令部收集、整理、评估和分发关于“飞碟”的信息。
鉴于成本、雷达覆盖不足以及难以追踪民用报告，维持战斗机持续警戒以拦截UAP的提议被认为不可行。
提及霍顿兄弟设计的德国“飞翼”以及“Loedding飞碟”（LD-2），作为潜在解释或相关设计。
bust_explanations: For the Bakersfield incidents, the description of 'two objects falling to earth from unknown source' with 'smoke and debris' or 'on fire with red and black smoke' could suggest meteors/bolides breaking up in the atmosphere. This would explain the visual phenomena and the lack of physical wreckage if the objects disintegrated completely before impact.

For the Hobson, Ohio sighting, a 'round, phosphorescent object' with 'great speed' and a 'phosphorescent trail' could be a high-altitude meteor or a re-entering piece of space debris reflecting sunlight, creating a phosphorescent appearance.

For the Bakersfield incidents, a conventional aircraft crash is a possibility given the 'falling aircraft' description. However, the document explicitly states that 'searching parties, aircraft and rescue units have made numerous attempts to locate these reported objects without success,' which strongly resists this explanation unless the crash site was simply never found or the objects were not conventional aircraft.
bust_explanations_zh: 关于贝克斯菲尔德事件，对“两个从未知来源坠落至地面的物体”伴有“烟雾和碎片”或“着火并冒着红黑烟雾”的描述，可能表明流星/火流星在大气层中解体。如果物体在撞击前完全解体，这将解释视觉现象和缺乏物理残骸的情况。
关于俄亥俄州霍布森的目击事件，一个“圆形、发磷光物体”伴有“高速”和“磷光轨迹”，可能是一个高空流星或一块反射阳光的再入太空碎片，从而产生磷光外观。
关于贝克斯菲尔德事件，鉴于“坠落飞行器”的描述，常规飞机坠毁是一种可能性。然而，文件明确指出“搜寻队、飞机和救援单位已多次尝试寻找这些报告的物体，但均未成功”，这强烈抵制了这种解释，除非坠机地点根本未被找到或这些物体并非常规飞机。
cover_up_indicators: T-2 Report 'German Flying Wings Designed by Horten Brothers' explicitly 'retained by this office' from recipient (Page 4)
Strict control over 'Loedding Flying Disc' drawing, requiring 'record be kept of all persons reviewing this drawing by witnessing with signatures and dates' (Page 5)
Discussion of highly sensitive intelligence regarding Russian development of jet-propelled aircraft based on German designs in context of 'Flying Discs' (Pages 5-6)
Reference to proposal for 'certain fighter aircraft be maintained on a continuous alert status... to aid in gathering information on Flying Discs' (Page 17)
cover_up_indicators_zh: 文件通篇标有“秘密”和“机密”字样
T-2报告“霍顿兄弟设计的德国飞翼”被明确“本办公室从接收方处保留”（第4页）
对“Loedding飞碟”图纸的严格控制，要求“所有查阅此图纸的人员必须签名并注明日期以作记录”（第5页）
在“飞碟”背景下，讨论关于俄罗斯基于德国设计开发喷气式飞机的极敏感情报（第5-6页）
提及一项“非正式提议，即某些战斗机应保持持续警戒状态……以协助收集飞碟信息”（第17页）
